authoritative_sources.title: GSA P100 Submittal Matrix (2024) - Version 2.0
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is a federal public owner and its submittal matrix is authoritative for architecture-and-engineering design-phase deliverables, including what narratives, calculations, specifications, drawings, and model artifacts are expected at each submission stage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: design-stage intake, deliverable identity validation, and package-completeness review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains normal client-status and missing-prerequisite scenarios so the lane asks for the stage, discipline, issue purpose, and expected artifact set before claiming a package is ready.

authoritative_sources.title: GSA BIM Guide Series 07 - Building Elements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's BIM guide is an official public-owner reference describing model-content expectations, element-level organization, and owner-facing digital deliverable discipline for A&E submissions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: digital package validation and model-aware client communication
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps BIM-related requests grounded in named model artifacts, issue purposes, and owner deliverable expectations instead of reducing them to generic file-sharing support.

authoritative_sources.title: Architect-Engineer Guide General Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Sacramento District
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE is a public contracting authority for architect-engineer services. This guide is authoritative for A-E deliverable schedules, QC certification, response-to-comments handling, and consultation with the client activity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: comment-resolution, QA or QC evidence review, and client-facing status updates
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Drives repeat-failure and recalled-package scenarios by requiring tracked comments, QC evidence, and coordination history before the lane can say a package is reissued or complete.

authoritative_sources.title: FC 1-300-09N Navy and Marine Corps Design Procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAVFAC is an official public-owner design authority. Its design procedures are authoritative for electronic design deliverables, source-document folders, final submissions, and record-drawing package requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: final issue, record-document assembly, and turnover packaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes the turnover and record-package scenarios so the lane distinguishes interim issue packages from final or record deliverables and records the required supporting folders.

authoritative_sources.title: Project BIM Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS publishes NBIMS-US, a recognized U.S. national BIM standard. Project BIM Requirements is authoritative for documenting owner goals, required BIM deliverables, exchange expectations, quality management, and legal constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: owner requirements interpretation and deliverable-planning baseline
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to ask for owner requirements, exchange definitions, and tenant BIM rules before promising models or data drops to a client.

authoritative_sources.title: BIM Execution Planning (BEP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NBIMS-US BEP is a recognized workflow standard for defining BIM information exchanges, coordination meetings, roles, responsibilities, and deliverable schedules in architecture-and-engineering projects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coordination-state review and common-data-environment workflow control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports scenarios where the lane must determine whether a model package is client-ready, still in coordination, or belongs with BIM management rather than generic support.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction to Operations Building Information Exchange (COBie) V3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS authors the COBie standard, which is authoritative for digital handover information covering spaces, products, equipment, as-builts, O&M, and commissioning information for facility turnover.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: turnover package evidence review and owner handoff communication
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Grounds the in-scope turnover scenario in actual asset-information, O&M, commissioning, and record-package content rather than a generic final-delivery claim.

authoritative_sources.title: NCARB Model Law and Model Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCARB is the recognized U.S. model-law body for architectural licensure. Its model law and regulations are authoritative for responsible control and architect seal boundaries that define what this lane must not represent as final or approved.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: seal-status screening, responsible-control boundary handling, and refusal logic
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces escalation when the client request depends on final architectural approval, signing, or sealing rather than administrative deliverable handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Guide to Engineering & Land Surveying for City and County Officials
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Board for Professional Engineers, Land Surveyors, and Geologists
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BPELSG is an official state engineering regulator. Its guide is authoritative for engineering-document preparation, signing, sealing, and the distinction between final professional documents and unsigned interim material.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: engineering-release boundary review and unsigned-interim-document handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from re-labeling draft or interim engineering packages as final, sealed, or permit-ready when the responsible engineer has not completed the required release step.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and deliverable identity validation
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, confirm project identity, client, design stage, discipline, issue purpose, requested date, and authoritative systems of record before any status answer is produced.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Package completeness and revision traceability review
workflow_stages.description: Review the deliverable register, transmittal log, issue history, comment matrix, file package, and model metadata to determine what can be described confidently as current, issued, recalled, blocked, or pending.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Stakeholder communication and supported resolution
workflow_stages.description: Prepare a client-safe resolution note, communication summary, or request-resolution summary that preserves architecture-and-engineering terminology and cites blocker state, assumptions, and next owner.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Seal, issue-purpose, and final-release boundary screening
workflow_stages.description: Stop, revise, or escalate communication when seal status, responsible control, issue purpose, jurisdiction, or release authority materially changes whether a package may be represented as final, permit-ready, bid-ready, or construction-ready.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Turnover, repeat-failure, and ownership-transfer escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package repeat issuance failures, recalled packages, conflicting records, security-control issues, turnover gaps, or adjacent-lane requests for the correct owner rather than improvising cross-functional authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: deliverable registers, submittal matrices, transmittal logs, and issue registers
drawing sets, specification books, narratives, calculations, reports, and response-to-comments matrices
basis-of-design documents, model issue records, native model references, clash reports, and review exports
quality-control certifications, release approvals, seal-status evidence, and client communication history
record drawings, source-document folders, COBie or asset-information exports, systems-manual sections, O&M binders, and commissioning reports
escalation packets, recall notices, and tenant policy extracts for release, security, and communication handling
systems_of_record: electronic document management system or common data environment
review-comment and QA or QC workflow system
approval registry for release, seal, and exception decisions

allowed: Classify client-deliverable requests as supported, blocked on missing prerequisites, or escalated because authority is outside the lane.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to establish current deliverable state safely.
Summarize authoritative register, transmittal, issue-history, BIM, and handover records in stakeholder-safe resolution notes and communication summaries.
Prepare escalation packets for repeat failures, recalled packages, security-control issues, or adjacent-lane ownership transfer.
escalate: Seal status, responsible professional ownership, issue-purpose rules, or jurisdictional release requirements materially change the answer.
The request requires design interpretation, code or permit interpretation, contractual commitment, fee adjustment, commercial approval, or final professional signoff.
Public guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, and current system-of-record status materially conflict on what may be issued or represented.
Repeat issuance failures, recalled packages, or turnover gaps indicate a QA or QC, BIM-management, document-control, or project-management problem beyond lane authority.
refuse: Represent a draft, recalled, untracked, or unsigned package as final, sealed, permit-ready, bid-ready, or construction-ready when authoritative records do not support that statement.
Issue attachments outside the authoritative transmittal or common-data-environment path as if they were controlled deliverables.
Provide architect-of-record or engineer-of-record judgment, code interpretation, seal decisions, legal interpretation, or contractual approval.

domain_failure_modes: sending the client an outdated drawing or specification revision because the response ignored the transmittal log or issue register
telling the client a package is final or permit-ready even though seal status, release approval, or responsible-control evidence is incomplete
treating BIM or COBie turnover like generic file delivery and failing to verify owner project requirements, exchange definitions, or asset-information completeness
answering a design-content or code-interpretation question inside a service lane instead of escalating to the architect or engineer of record
issuing screenshots, local exports, or email attachments outside the authoritative CDE or EDMS path and breaking traceability
closing a repeat-failure or recalled-package case without preserving the issuance trail, QA or QC notes, and next-owner escalation package

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Architecture and engineering seal mechanics vary materially by jurisdiction and by whether a package is client-review, permit, bid, construction, record, or archive issue. The pack therefore requires tenant and jurisdiction context whenever seal status changes the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific stage names, security controls, and system labels
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenants may use SD or DD or CD, 30 or 60 or 90 or 100, IFA or IFB or IFC, or secure EDMS-specific issue codes. The pack maps those variants back to the public baseline rather than assuming one naming scheme.

notes: Research output constrains this lane to administrative, evidence-backed client deliverable handling and explicitly avoids architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, permit, legal, or commercial authority.

The public baseline emphasizes revision control, release discipline, and handover completeness because those are the material differences between architecture-and-engineering deliverables and generic client service work.
